value.claim: Replacing large spatial convolutions (e.g., 5x5) with sequences of smaller convolutions (e.g., two 3x3 layers) reduces computational cost and parameters while maintaining expressiveness.
Factorizing asymmetric convolutions (e.g., 3x3 into 1x7 followed by 7x1) on medium grid sizes provides significant computational savings compared to symmetric factorizations.
A new efficient grid size reduction module that combines pooling and convolution in parallel to avoid representational bottlenecks while reducing feature map dimensions.
Label smoothing regularization, which replaces one-hot ground truth labels with a mixture including a uniform distribution, consistently improves top-1 and top-5 error rates.
Auxiliary classifiers act primarily as regularizers rather than gradient propagators, especially when their fully connected layers are batch-normalized.

The proposed Inception-v3 architecture achieves 21.2% top-1 and 5.6% top-5 error on ILSVRC 2012 validation set with single-frame evaluation, outperforming prior state-of-the-art methods like VGGNet and He et al.'s PReLU network at a fraction of the computational cost.
Ensembling four Inception-v3 models with multi-crop evaluation achieves 3.5% top-5 error on ILSVRC 2012, representing a significant reduction over previous ensemble results.
